pr.title: grdisplay cleanup and near match of grDisplay_801C5DB0
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Cleaned up grdisplay and rewrote grDisplay_801C5B90 plus grDisplay_801C5DB0 into more typed, structured C, producing a near match for grDisplay_801C5DB0. The PR also tightened Ground_801C1E84's return type from void* to HSD_GObj* and replaced a local HSD_CObj view-matrix helper in jobj.c with the existing HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtrDirect accessor. The decomp.dev bot reported grDisplay_801C5DB0 improving from 0.00% to 99.58%, grDisplay_801C5B90 from 99.08% to 99.85%, and grdisplay .data reaching 100%.
what_changed: Added grdisplay-specific and supporting includes in grdisplay.c, including grdisplay.h, inlines.h, lb/forward.h, baselib/fog.h, and baselib/state.h.
Changed grDisplay_801C5B90's signature from HSD_JObj*, void*, u32, int to HSD_JObj*, Mtx, u32 flags, u32 rendermode, making the view matrix and render mode usage explicit.

Reworked grDisplay_801C5B90 control flow around JOBJ_INSTANCE, JOBJ_HIDDEN, HSD_JObjSetupMatrix, HSD_MtxInverseConcat, HSD_CObjGetCurrent, PSMTXConcat, recursive child traversal, fighter shadow state updates, lbShadow_8000EFEC, and HSD_JObjDisp.

Introduced a decompiled near-match body for grDisplay_801C5DB0 with ground visibility/camera gating, current camera checks, fog handling, state invalidation, stage shadow setup, JObj callback dispatch, and stage fog restoration.
Used GET_GROUND, GET_COBJ, GET_JOBJ, and GET_FOG-style access macros in grDisplay_801C5DB0 to express HSD_GObj payload access more concretely.
Updated Ground_801C1E84 in ground.c and ground.h to return HSD_GObj* instead of void*, matching its use as a fog gobj source via stage_info.x12C.
Removed a local inline HSD_CObjGetViewMtx helper from jobj.c and replaced its use with HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtrDirect(cobj) in HSD_JObjDispAll.
smart_moves: Typed Ground_801C1E84 as HSD_GObj* before using its result as a fog object, avoiding repeated casts or weak void* reasoning in display code.
Reused HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtrDirect rather than preserving a redundant local inline accessor, aligning jobj.c with the baselib cobj API and improving consistency.
Kept the instance-JObj path in grDisplay_801C5B90 close to the existing HSD_JObjDispAll pattern: setup matrices, assert current camera, concatenate viewing matrix, then recurse into children.
Expressed render selection using named JObj flag constants such as JOBJ_INSTANCE and JOBJ_HIDDEN instead of leaving the logic as raw values like 0x1000 and 0x10.

Modeled shadow rendering side effects explicitly by iterating HSD_GObj_Entities->fighters, querying fighter positions with ftLib_80086644, toggling LbShadow.x0_b4, and calling lbShadow routines before display.
Used early returns and a labeled skip path in grDisplay_801C5DB0 to represent camera/current-object gating while preserving the matched control-flow shape.
decomp_lessons: When a stage helper returns a known HSD_GObj from stage_info, update the function prototype in both .c and .h; stronger return types can unlock cleaner display/fog decompilation.
For JObj display recursion, matching code may require preserving matrix stack shape with Mtx locals and using HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtrDirect rather than ad hoc field accessors.

Stage display functions often intertwine camera state, Ground flags, fog state, JObj callbacks, and fighter shadow side effects; matching one function may require correctly naming and typing all of those interactions.
Bitfield names on Ground flags, such as gp->x11_flags.b012 and gp->x10_flags.b2/b3/b5, can make large display-condition blocks understandable without fully naming the flags yet.
Near matches can still provide substantial project-wide progress: the bot reported grDisplay_801C5DB0 at 99.58%, grDisplay_801C5B90 at 99.85%, and grdisplay .data at 100% after this cleanup.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based names for not-yet-renamed ground/display functions, including grDisplay_801C5B90, grDisplay_801C5DB0, Ground_801C1E84, Ground_801C2C8C, Ground_801C1E18, and Ground_801C1E84.
Used local names that reflect role rather than register ancestry: gp, camgobj, foggobj, fog, fighter, shadow, cobj, mtx, vec, flags, and rendermode.
Preferred typed HSD object names and macros such as HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, HSD_CObj, HSD_Fog, GET_GROUND, GET_COBJ, GET_JOBJ, and GET_FOG.
Used baselib-style accessor naming HSD_CObjGetViewingMtxPtrDirect instead of introducing or retaining HSD_CObjGetViewMtx.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed the grDisplay_801C5B90 second parameter to Mtx and threaded it through recursive calls and HSD_JObjDisp, matching the view-matrix usage directly.
Preserved recursive traversal structure for both instance JObjs and child JObjs, with separate flag tests using flags shifted by 18 and 28.
Matched matrix-concat behavior by using HSD_MtxInverseConcat followed by PSMTXConcat with the current camera viewing matrix.
Used an explicit HSD_ASSERT after HSD_CObjGetCurrent in the matrix path, mirroring the expected baselib display assertion behavior.
In grDisplay_801C5DB0, represented difficult branch structure with early returns plus a goto skip label, a practical tactic when matching camera gating and special-case current-object checks.
Included the suspicious OSReport("oioi... %08x\n") branch around gp->x18 pointer high-bit testing as observed in the diff rather than over-normalizing it away.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only issue comment was an automated decomp.dev report showing matched code +5868 bytes and matched data +832 bytes for GALE01.
The bot reported grdisplay .data reaching 100%, grDisplay_801C5DB0 improving to 99.58%, and grDisplay_801C5B90 improving to 99.85%.
The bot also reported many grzebes matches/improvements, but the changed-files evidence in this slice is limited to grdisplay, ground, ground.h, and jobj.c.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match or rename grDisplay_801C5DB0.
Search for additional uses of Ground_801C1E84 to confirm the HSD_GObj* return type and fog-object semantics.
Compare HSD_JObjDispAll and grDisplay_801C5B90 recursion patterns for reusable matrix matching tactics.
Investigate whether the OSReport("oioi... %08x\n") call in grDisplay_801C5DB0 has a missing argument or requires a matching-specific workaround.
Look for later cleanup of gp->x10_flags.b2/b3/b5 and gp->x11_flags.b012 into named Ground flag fields.
